expectations.text: a markdown report file is written somewhere the user can read (the assistant's final message names the path)
the report file is non-empty (at least 800 bytes)
./skills/bad-skill/SKILL.md is byte-identical to the input fixture (read-only audit)
the report has an Idempotency section (heading or labeled findings list)
the report has a Reproducibility section
the report has a Context management section
the report has a Strict definitions section
the report has a Security section (heading or labeled findings list); for this fixture it is expected to contain 'No findings.' since the bad-skill does not handle credentials
a finding mentions that the description has no 'when to skip' / negative case (under strict-definitions)
at least one finding cites a specific vague phrase from the bad skill ('as needed', 'appropriately', 'reasonably', or 'use your judgment')
a finding mentions that `date` is used without being declared as an input (under reproducibility)
a finding flags `gh pr create` for missing a precondition or idempotency declaration
a finding flags the long inline output template as content that should move to references/ (under context-management)
a finding notes that the description ('Generate release notes from git history.') does not mention the workflow's side effects — pushing a tag and creating a PR — under strict-definitions
at least 80% of findings cite a `file:line` reference
the report does not use severity tiers (no Critical/High/Medium/Low/P0/P1 labels)
findings are grouped by objective (the five objective names appear as headings or list-section labels), not by severity
expectations.evidence: objective sections present: Idempotency, Reproducibility, Context management, Strict definitions, Security

expectations.text: a markdown report file is written and the path is named in the assistant's final message
./skills/word-count/SKILL.md is byte-identical to the input fixture
total finding count across all five objectives is ≤ 4 (the skill is genuinely clean — over-flagging it would mean the audit is noisy)
a finding catches the description ↔ workflow scope mismatch: description says 'or set of files' but argument-hint and workflow are single-file (under strict-definitions)
the report has a 'Passing checks' / 'Passed' section that lists at least 3 things the skill got right
the report does NOT raise a finding for missing idempotency declaration (because the skill declares it on line 11: 'This skill is idempotent')
the report does NOT raise a finding for missing 'when to skip' (the description includes 'Skip when the user wants more sophisticated text analysis...')
the Security section reports no findings (the skill handles only file paths, not credentials — the audit must not manufacture security findings out of unrelated text)
the report does not use severity tiers
the assistant's transcript / final message indicates the skill was resolved at ./.claude/skills/word-count/ (proving the name-based resolver worked)
a report file is written and its path is named in the final message
./.claude/skills/word-count/SKILL.md is byte-identical to the input fixture
a markdown report file is written and the path is named in the assistant's final message
./skills/mongo-explorer/SKILL.md and ./skills/mongo-explorer/references/database.md are byte-identical to the input fixtures
the report has a Security section with at least 3 findings (the fixture exhibits four distinct security violations)

a finding under Security flags the instruction to prompt the user for the password (e.g. 'prompt them for the full connection string including the password' or 'prompt the user for their MongoDB password' or 'ask the user to paste their password')

a finding under Security flags the URL-form connection string carrying an embedded password — either the argument-hint `[connection-string]` whose protocol admits `user:password@`, or the example `mongodb://app_user:hunter2@...` in step 5

a finding under Security flags the discard-after-read pattern (e.g. 'After parsing the connection string, discard the password' or 'discard the password from the in-memory representation') — names this as too-late, since the secret was already in context

a finding under Security flags the generated `.env` containing a concrete `MONGO_PASSWORD=<password>` value, or the hardcoded fallback `MONGO_PASSWORD="${MONGO_PASSWORD:-hunter2}"` in the generated query.sh
Security findings cite `SKILL.md:<line>` or `references/database.md:<line>` — the author can jump straight to the offending line
a Security finding cites `references/database.md:<line>` and flags the prompting violation in that file (proves the audit recursed into the references/ directory rather than only scanning SKILL.md)
the report does not use severity tiers (security findings live under the Security objective heading, not under a 'Critical' or 'High' label)
the Passing checks section does NOT list Security as a passing check (the skill never demonstrates the env-var + refuse-and-instruct pattern)
a markdown report file is written and the path is named in the assistant's final message
./skills/implement-graphql-resolver/SKILL.md is byte-identical to the input fixture (read-only audit)
the report has a Context management section

a Context management finding flags that at least one phase spawns a subagent regardless of input size — i.e. there's no partitioning rule (per-section, per-N-items, or up-front scope gate). Wording will vary but should reference unbounded scope, missing partitioning, or per-call output growing with input.
expectations.evidence: context-mgmt finding mentions partitioning / unbounded scope

expectations.text: a Context management finding flags that `_context_schema.md` and/or `_context_resolvers.md` is described in open-ended prose — no strict format (one symbol per line, fixed schema, etc.) is specified
expectations.evidence: context-mgmt finding flags open-ended summary format

expectations.text: a Context management finding flags the absence of a hard line cap on the inter-phase summary files (the finding may be combined with the format finding above as long as the cap concern is explicitly named)

a Context management finding flags that a later phase Reads a source file an earlier phase mutated — either Phase 2 reading `src/schema.ts` in full after Phase 1 edited it, or Phase 3 reading `src/resolvers.ts` in full after Phase 2 edited it. At least one of the two must be cited.
expectations.evidence: context-mgmt finding flags re-read of mutated source

expectations.text: Context management findings cite `SKILL.md:<line>` — the author can jump to the offending phase description
the report does NOT raise a finding for missing idempotency declaration (the skill declares it explicitly on line 12: 'This skill is idempotent')
the report does NOT raise a finding for missing 'when to skip' (the description includes 'Skip when the user wants schema-only changes...')
the Security section reports no findings (the skill handles no credentials — the audit must not manufacture security findings out of unrelated text)
the report does not use severity tiers (no Critical/High/Medium/Low/P0/P1 labels)
findings are grouped by objective, not by severity
expectations.evidence: objective sections present: Idempotency, Reproducibility, Context management, Strict definitions, Security

expectations.text: a markdown report file is written and the path is named in the assistant's final message
./skills/code-review-checklist/SKILL.md is byte-identical to the input fixture (read-only audit)
the report has a Context management section

the report does NOT raise a Check 6 / per-subagent unbounded scope finding — the skill is phased main-thread with no delegation, and Check 6 is scoped to delegated workflows. Findings about main-thread phasing (e.g. 'phase X spawns one subagent regardless of input size', 'no partitioning rule', 'per-call output growing with input') would be false positives
expectations.evidence: no Check 6 finding patterns matched in Context management section

expectations.text: the report does NOT raise a finding for missing idempotency declaration (the fixture declares it explicitly on line 12: 'This skill is idempotent')
the report does NOT raise a finding for missing 'when to skip' (the description includes 'Skip when the user wants a security audit, a performance review, or feedback on more than one PR at a time')
the Security section reports no findings — the skill handles only file paths and diff content, not credentials
total finding count across all five objectives is ≤ 4 (the skill is genuinely clean — over-flagging would mean the audit is noisy)
the report does not use severity tiers (no Critical/High/Medium/Low/P0/P1 labels)
findings are grouped by objective, not by severity
expectations.evidence: objective sections present: Idempotency, Reproducibility, Context management, Strict definitions, Security
